heading: Waiver of navigation and vessel-inspection laws
label: 501 Waiver of navigation and vessel-inspection laws
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)50146 App.:3 note prec., §§ 1, 2, .Dec. 27, 1950, ch. 115564 Stat. 1120In subsection (b), the words “When the head of an agency . . . considers it necessary” are substituted for “either upon his own initiative or upon the written recommendation of the head of any other Government agency, when he deems that such action is necessary” to eliminate unnecessary words.

Amendments2022—Subsec. (b)(1). , added par. (1) and struck out former par. (1). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “When the head of an agency responsible for the administration of the navigation or vessel-inspection laws considers it necessary in the interest of national defense, the individual, following a determination by the Maritime Administrator, acting in the Administrator’s capacity as Director, National Shipping Authority, of the non-availability of qualified United States flag capacity to meet national defense requirements, may waive compliance with those laws to the extent, in the manner, and on the terms the individual, in consultation with the Administrator, acting in that capacity, prescribes.”Pub. L. 117–263, § 3541(1)(A)Subsec. (b)(2)(B). , substituted “determination referred to in paragraph (1)(A)” for “determinations referred to in paragraph (1)”.Pub. L. 117–263, § 3541(1)(B)Subsec. (b)(3)(A). , added subpar. (A) and struck out former subpar. (A). Prior to amendment, subpar. (A) read as follows: “for each determination referred to in paragraph (1), identify any actions that could be taken to enable qualified United States flag capacity to meet national defense requirements;”.Pub. L. 117–263, § 3541(1)(C)Subsec. (b)(5), (6). , added pars. (5) and (6).Pub. L. 117–263, § 3541(1)(D)Subsec. (c)(1). , inserted “and the individual requesting such waiver (if not the owner or operator of the vessel)” before “shall submit” in introductory provisions, added subpars. (B), (E), and (F), and redesignated former subpars. (B), (C), and (D) as (C), (D), and (G), respectively.Pub. L. 117–263, § 3541(2)2021—Subsec. (a). , added subsec. (a) and struck out former subsec. (a). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “On request of the Secretary of Defense, the head of an agency responsible for the administration of the navigation or vessel-inspection laws shall waive compliance with those laws to the extent the Secretary considers necessary in the interest of national defense.”Pub. L. 116–283, § 3502(a)(1)(A)Subsec. (b)(2), (3). , (ii), added par. (2) and redesignated former par. (2) as (3). Former par. (3) redesignated (4).Pub. L. 116–283, § 3502(a)(1)(B)(i)Subsec. children.heading: On Request of Secretary of Defense.—
children.label: a On Request of Secretary of Defense.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: On request of the Secretary of Defense, the head of an agency responsible for the administration of the navigation or vessel-inspection laws shall waive compliance with those laws to the extent the Secretary considers necessary in the interest of national defense to address an immediate adverse effect on military operations.

children.heading: .—Submittal of explanation to congress
children.label: 2 .—Submittal of explanation to congress
children.text: Not later than 24 hours after making a request under paragraph (1), the Secretary of Defense shall submit to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure and the Committee on Armed Services of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation and the Committee on Armed Services of the Senate a written explanation of the circumstances requiring such a waiver in the interest of national defense, including a confirmation that there are insufficient qualified vessels to meet the needs of national defense without such a waiver.

children.label: A
children.text: following a determination in accordance with the requirements of paragraph (3) by the Maritime Administrator, acting in the Administrator’s capacity as Director, National Shipping Authority, of the non-availability of qualified United States flag capacity to meet national defense requirements;

children.label: B
children.text: not earlier than 48 hours after a waiver request is published under paragraph (6)(A); and

children.label: C
children.text: on a vessel specific basis to the extent, in the manner, and on the terms the head of such agency, in consultation with the Administrator, acting in such capacity, prescribes.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraphs (B) and (C), a waiver issued under this subsection shall be for a period of not more than 10 days.

children.heading: .—Waiver extension
children.label: B .—Waiver extension
children.text: Upon the termination of the period of a waiver issued under this subsection, the head of an agency may extend the waiver for an additional period of not more than 10 days, if the Maritime Administrator makes the determination referred to in paragraph (1)(A).

children.heading: .—Aggregate duration
children.label: C .—Aggregate duration
children.text: The aggregate duration of the period of all waivers and extensions of waivers under this subsection with respect to any one set of events shall not exceed 45 days.

children.label: i
children.text: identify any actions that could be taken to enable qualified United States flag capacity to meet national defense requirements prior to the issuance of a waiver; and

children.label: ii
children.text: not assess the non-availability of qualified United States flag capacity to meet national defense requirements retrospectively after the date on which a waiver is requested;

children.label: B
children.text: provide notice of each such determination to the Secretary of Transportation and the head of the agency referred to in paragraph (1) for which the determination is made; and

children.label: C
children.text: publish each such determination on the Internet Web site of the Department of Transportation not later than 48 hours after notice of the determination is provided to the Secretary of Transportation.

children.label: i
children.text: of any request for a waiver of the navigation or vessel-inspection laws under this section not later than 48 hours after receiving such a request; and

children.label: ii
children.text: of the issuance of any such waiver not later than 48 hours after such issuance.

children.label: i
children.text: the reasons the waiver is necessary; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the reasons actions referred to in paragraph (3)(A) are not feasible.

children.heading: .—Notification required for extensions
children.label: C .—Notification required for extensions
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, an extension requested or issued under paragraph (2)(B) shall be treated in the same manner as a waiver requested or issued under this subsection.

children.heading: .—Prospective application
children.label: 5 .—Prospective application
children.text: No waiver shall be issued for a vessel if, at the time of the waiver request under this section, such vessel is laden with merchandise that, pursuant to the requested waiver, could be unladen at points or places to which the coastwise laws apply.

children.heading: .—Publication of waiver requests
children.label: A .—Publication of waiver requests
children.text: Upon receiving a request for a waiver under this subsection, the head of an agency referred to in paragraph (1) shall publish such request on the website of such agency.

children.heading: .—Publication of waiver denial
children.label: B .—Publication of waiver denial
children.text: Not later than 48 hours after denying a waiver requested under this subsection, the head of an agency referred to in paragraph (1) shall publish on the website of such agency an explanation for denying such waiver, including applicable findings to support the denial.

children.label: A
children.text: the name and flag of the vessel;

children.label: B
children.text: the name of the owner and operator of the vessel;

children.label: C
children.text: the dates of the voyage;

children.label: D
children.text: any relevant ports of call;

children.label: E
children.text: a description of the cargo carried;

children.label: F
children.text: an explanation as to why the waiver was in the interest of national defense; and

children.label: G
children.text: any other information the Maritime Administrator determines necessary.

children.heading: .—Publication
children.label: 2 .—Publication
children.text: Not later than 48 hours after receiving a report under paragraph (1), the Maritime Administrator shall publish such report on an appropriate website of the Department of Transportation.

children.heading: .—Termination of Authority
children.label: d .—Termination of Authority
children.text: The authority granted by this section shall terminate at such time as the Congress by concurrent resolution or the President may designate.
